app.title: Portfolio
app.subtitle: Portfolio analysis
evolution.title: Evolution over time
evolution.need_more: Import at least two snapshots to see an evolution curve.
evolution.insight.total_clients: Total portfolio size: every client on file, active or not. A rising curve means you're winning more clients than you lose.
evolution.insight.active_clients: Clients with at least one live policy. This is the real base of your activity — the gap with the total measures portfolio erosion.
evolution.insight.total_polices: Total volume of live contracts. Rising faster than client count means more multi-equipment per client.
evolution.insight.avg_polices: Average policies per active client. A key loyalty indicator: the higher it goes, the more attached your clients are to your office.
evolution.insight.total_premium: Annual revenue carried by the portfolio. Compare with client growth to see if you're moving upmarket or just adding volume.
evolution.insight.avg_premium: Average premium per active client. Measures unit value: a drop signals churn on big accounts or concentration on small contracts.
evolution.insight.total_sinistres: Claims reported in the current year. Watch it to spot risky branches and adjust pricing.
evolution.insight.mono_policy_clients: Clients with a single policy — your prime cross-sell target. A drop means your follow-up campaigns are working.
home.title: Portfolio
home.empty.title: No snapshots
home.empty.desc: Import an Excel export to start the analysis.
upload.title: New snapshot
upload.files.hint: Select the CLIENTS, POLICES, COMPAGNIES and SINISTRES files exported from the broker software.
upload.error.no_files: Please select at least one file.
preview.title: Preview
dashboard.title: Dashboard
dash.s1_desc: Portfolio composition between individuals and businesses, and clients without an active policy.
dash.s2_desc: Geographic and demographic breakdown of clients: postal codes, ages, gender, marital status, social status.
dash.s3_desc: Fields with missing or unknown values. Critical fields (age, gender, marital status, phone, email) are highlighted.
dash.s4_desc: Policy distribution by branch, per-client penetration, single-policy clients, and breakdown by insurer.
dash.s5_desc: Commercial segments with upside, derived from the portfolio data.
dash.s5_cross_sell: Clients with a single policy: cross-sell opportunities.
dash.s5_succession: Clients 60+ without Life or Investment: succession planning.
dash.s5_young_families: P&C clients without Life: young families to cover.
dash.s5_high_value: Clients with 5 or more policies: priority retention.
dash.s5_dq_cleanup: Client records to complete (critical fields missing).
report.title: Report: Insurance Portfolio Analysis
report.s1_intro: Split between individuals and businesses among active clients (with at least one policy), and detail of clients without a policy.
report.s2_postal_info: Postal codes where 70% of clients are located, listed in descending order. Remaining codes are grouped under "Out of zone".
report.s3_sex_info: Male / Female breakdown among Individual clients. Clients without recorded gender are counted under "Unknown". Percentages are computed on clients with known gender.
report.s3_age_info: Age brackets computed from birth date and reference year. The 60+ rows identify clients with succession and investment potential.
report.s3_civil_info: Declared marital status for Individual clients. Percentages are computed on clients with known status.
report.s3_social_info: Declared social status. Percentages are computed on clients with known status; a large "Unknown" share indicates a field to complete in priority.
report.s4_dq_info: Share of missing fields per attribute. Critical fields (>50% missing) are in red. Email and phone are always considered critical because they are needed for outreach.
report.s5_branches_info: Policy distribution by insurance branch. The bar chart visualizes the share of each branch; the pie chart aggregates P&C, Life, Investment and Credit.
report.s5_subscription_info: Share of active clients holding at least one policy in each branch. A client may be counted in multiple branches.
report.s5_distribution_info: Number of policies held per client. A high number of single-policy clients signals cross-selling potential.
report.s5_mono_detail_info: Single-policy clients broken down by the branch of their sole policy.
report.s5_companies_info: Insurer penetration within the portfolio. Variants of the same insurer (AXA Belgium/AXA, ALLIANZ/ALLIANZ Assistance, etc.) are grouped together.
report.s6_info: Summary of key portfolio ratios. "Known" percentages are computed on the total of active clients.
report.s5_subscription_note: (clients holding at least one policy in this branch)
report.ratio.total_with_police: Clients with at least one policy
settings.title: Settings
settings.profile.title: Profile
settings.profile.user_hint: Contact details for the user viewing this portfolio.
settings.profile.name: Full name
settings.profile.company_hint: Portfolio holder information. Shown in the sidebar and reports.
settings.profile.locale_hint: Language is stored in your profile.
settings.passphrase.title: Passphrase
settings.passphrase.hint: Protects the backups you place in iCloud/Drive. Without it, backups cannot be restored. Keep it in your password manager.
settings.backup.title: Encrypted backup
settings.backup.include_profile_prompt:
Include your profile (name, company, logo) in the backup?

OK = include profile. Cancel = portfolio data only.
settings.backup.import_profile_prompt: This backup contains a user/company profile. Replace the local profile with it?
settings.sync.title: Cloud sync
settings.sync.link_hint: Pick a .ptf file inside iCloud Drive, OneDrive, or Dropbox. Every save is written to it automatically.
settings.sync.unlink_confirm: Unlink this file? The file itself is not deleted.
settings.sync.share_hint: Safari cannot write to iCloud automatically. Use the iOS share sheet to save the backup into Files → iCloud Drive after each update, then import it on the other device.
settings.sync.share_to_icloud_hint: Opens the iOS share sheet. Choose 'Save to Files' then iCloud Drive, overwriting your previous copy.
settings.sync.pull_from_icloud_hint: Opens the file picker. Choose the copy from iCloud Drive to restore the portfolio.
settings.help.title: Help
settings.help.tutorial_hint: A guided tour of the app.
settings.privacy.title: Privacy & security
settings.privacy.local_body: Portfolio data lives in your browser (SQLite via OPFS). No client data is sent to any server. Backups are encrypted (AES-256-GCM, PBKDF2 600,000 iterations).
settings.privacy.profile_body: Your name, email, company and logo are kept unencrypted in browser local storage for quick access. Use 'Reset the app' to wipe them.
settings.privacy.gdpr_body: You remain the data controller for imported data. Lock your device, use a strong backup passphrase, delete stale snapshots, and export only to trusted locations.
settings.storage.title: Storage
settings.update.title: Updates
settings.update.reload_hint: Force-fetches the latest code and assets. Your data is kept.
settings.update.reparse_all_hint: Re-runs the parser on every snapshot's original XLSX files. Use after a parser update.
settings.update.reparse_all_confirm: Re-parse every snapshot from its original XLSX files? Derived data will be replaced.
settings.danger.title: Danger zone
settings.danger.reset_hint: Erases all local data AND the PWA cache. Cannot be undone.
settings.danger.reset_confirm: Erase everything (data + cache)? This action cannot be undone.
dashboard.reparse_hint: Re-runs the parser on the original XLSX files. Use after a parser update.
dashboard.reparse_confirm: Re-parse this snapshot from its original XLSX files? Derived data will be replaced.
dashboard.reparse_no_files: No source files saved for this snapshot.
tutorial.title: Tutorial
tutorial.subtitle: A tour of the features. Tap a section to expand it.
tutorial.overview.title: What is Portefeuille for?
tutorial.overview.body:
Portefeuille analyses the Excel exports from your broker software (CLIENTS, POLICIES, INSURERS, CLAIMS) and gives you a clear picture of your book: composition, demographics, branches, data quality, cross-sell opportunities, evolution over time.

All analysis runs locally — no client data leaves your browser.
tutorial.upload.title: Import a snapshot
tutorial.upload.body:
From the home screen tap 'New snapshot'. Pick the date (the extraction date, not today's), then select your Excel files.

The app auto-detects each file and drops it in the right slot (Clients / Policies / Insurers / Claims). CLIENTS is required; the others are optional but enrich the analysis.

Tap 'Confirm' to persist the snapshot.
tutorial.dashboard.title: Snapshot dashboard
tutorial.dashboard.body:
Each snapshot shows the headline KPIs (clients, policies, premium, commission, claims) followed by detailed sections: composition, branches, insurers, geography, demographics, data quality, commercial opportunities.

The opportunity segments (mono-policy, succession, young families, high-value) point to the clients to work on first.

From the dashboard you can also export a summary xlsx or edit the snapshot date.
tutorial.evolution.title: Evolution over time
tutorial.evolution.body:
The 'Evolution' page compares all your snapshots and draws a line per metric (clients, policies, premium, claims, etc.).

Each card carries a short explainer: what the metric means and how to read it.

Evolution becomes useful from two snapshots onwards. Import regularly (quarterly or monthly) to see trends.
tutorial.snapshots.title: Managing snapshots
tutorial.snapshots.body:
Every import creates a dated snapshot. You can open, rename, fix the date, recompute (after a branch-mapping update) or delete them from the relevant dashboard.

The list lives in the left sidebar.
tutorial.profile.title: Your profile
tutorial.profile.body:
In settings, fill in your name, role and email, plus the company details (name, VAT, address, logo). They appear in the sidebar and in exports.

Language is part of the profile: it follows your preferences when you import one.
tutorial.backup.title: Encrypted backup (.ptf)
tutorial.backup.body:
'Export backup' produces an encrypted .ptf file (AES-256-GCM) protected by a passphrase. The legacy .portefeuille extension is still accepted on import.

On export you choose whether the profile (name + company + logo) is bundled in. On import, if the archive carries one, the app asks for confirmation before replacing the local profile.

IMPORTANT — browser data can be lost:
• when you clear cookies and site data,
• when the browser frees space under pressure (automatic quota),
• when you switch browsers or reset the device,
• in private browsing mode.

An up-to-date .ptf backup is your only safety net. Export regularly and keep it somewhere reliable (iCloud Drive, OneDrive, external disk).

Save the passphrase in your password manager (Apple Passwords, 1Password, Bitwarden…). Without it the backup is unrecoverable — there is no reset mechanism.
tutorial.sync.title: Sync across devices
tutorial.sync.body:
You can share your book across devices via iCloud Drive, OneDrive, Dropbox, or Google Drive.

On Chrome, Edge or Opera (desktop, Android):
1. Settings → 'Link a sync file'.
2. Navigate into your cloud folder in the file picker and create a file (e.g. portefeuille.ptf).
3. Choose a passphrase. The app rewrites that file after every save.
4. On the other device, restore the file via 'Restore from backup' then link it again from settings.

On iOS Safari:
1. Settings → 'Share to iCloud Drive'. The app prepares an encrypted .ptf and opens the iOS share sheet.
2. Choose 'Save to Files' → iCloud Drive. Overwrite the previous copy so the latest one is always there.
3. On the other device, open the app and use 'Pull from iCloud' to fetch the copy.

On Firefox (desktop): use manual export/import via Settings → Encrypted backup.
tutorial.privacy.title: Privacy, GDPR & data loss
tutorial.privacy.body:
All client data stays in your browser. Backups are encrypted (AES-256-GCM, keys derived via PBKDF2-SHA256 with 600,000 iterations).

Your profile (name, company, logo) is stored unencrypted for quick access. The sync passphrase is also stored locally — treat the browser as a professional tool and lock your session.

Heads up: browser storage is not permanent. Data can be wiped automatically (quota), on close (private browsing), after a system reinstall, or when switching browsers. The only reliable protection is an up-to-date .ptf backup with its passphrase saved in a password manager.

As a broker, you remain the data controller: restrict device access, delete stale snapshots, and export only to trusted locations.
tutorial.reset.title: Update or reset
tutorial.reset.body:
Settings → Updates → 'Reload latest version' forces the PWA to fetch the latest code without touching your data.

Settings → Danger zone → 'Reset the app' wipes all data AND the cache. Use when you hit persistent problems or before handing the device to someone else.
